title: Oxybaphus ovatus
intro: Oxybaphus ovatus, also known as the desert honeysuckle, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its fragrant, white flowers and gray-green foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Oxybaphus ovatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with desert honeysuckle include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Oxybaphus ovatus is classified within the family of evening primroses, known as the Oenotheraceae family. This classification places it within the order Myrtales, which is a group of flowering plants. At the rank of family, it is most commonly classified as Oenotheraceae. However, it can also be classified within the broader group known as the Myrtales clade. Further classification within the kingdom and other ranks is also applicable.
